task_completion.description: Blender is open and an object has the Ocean modifier applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender interface is visible. In the Properties editor under the Modifiers tab (wrench icon), an 'Ocean' modifier is added to the active object.

task_completion.description: The Ocean modifier's resolution is at least 10 and spatial size is between 20m and 200m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ocean modifier settings, the 'Resolution' (Viewport/Render) is set to 10 or higher, and the 'Spatial Size' is set to a value between 20 and 200.

task_completion.description: The world background is set to a reddish/orange color for a sunset atmosphere.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the World Properties tab (globe icon), the Background Color is set to a reddish or orange hue (Red value > Blue value).

task_completion.description: A Sun light is present and angled to simulate a low sunset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Sun light is selected in the Outliner. In the Object Properties (orange square icon), the Rotation values indicate a low angle (not pointing straight down).

task_completion.description: The active camera is positioned at a height between 0.5m and 15m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Camera is selected. In the Object Properties tab, the Z Location is between 0.5m and 15m.

task_completion.description: The rendered image and project file are saved in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or Blender's File Browser shows 'ocean_render.png' and 'ocean_scene.blend' saved in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Blender GUI to create the scene and render the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through Blender menus, adjusting properties in panels, and initiating the render via the GUI, rather than just running a pre-written Python script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The rendered image is a genuine output from Blender.
integrity.visual_evidence: The render window or image viewer shows a 3D ocean scene consistent with the modifier settings and lighting, not a downloaded photograph.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply write a script to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen interacting with the 3D viewport and properties panels, not just typing code in a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using Blender's Ocean modifier with Resolution >= 10 and Spatial Size between 20 and 200. The world background must be reddish/orange (Red > Blue). A Sun light must be used at a low angle (Z-direction component > -0.9, not pointing straight down). The camera Z-location must be between 0.5m and 15.0m. The final render must be saved as /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ocean_render.png (min 50 KB) and the project as /home/ga/BlenderProjects/ocean_scene.blend.
